purpose: Count remaining feats in the broader combat-mechanics implementation queue, not only the literal Combat taxonomy bucket.

methodology.strictCombatBucket: Only feats under bucketSummary.Combat in the generated taxonomy report.
methodology.broadActionableCombatMechanics: Feats whose taxonomy subbucket strongly implies combat runtime work: attack/damage roll options, defenses, reactions, full attacks, grapples, combat movement/action riders, vehicle/gunnery, damage threshold/condition, second wind/combat recovery, armor/proficiency, droid combat/systems, combat teamwork, and combat equipment/demolitions. This excludes ambiguous Force/species/social/skill feats unless already demarcated implemented in the addendum or part of one of the counted subbuckets.
methodology.implementedOverride: The implementation status addendum is treated as authoritative over older generated backlog statuses. Its 23 implemented/corrected feats are subtracted from the broad queue.

notCountedInBroadActionableTotalYet.reason: These areas contain many combat-looking feats, but the taxonomy alone is not enough to mark them actionable without source text or a finer implementation-home audit. They should be reviewed in the next pass rather than counted as ready implementation backlog.
examples: Force feats with combat names but unclear force-point/power interactions
Species & Origin feats with military/combat flavor but mixed social, skill, and passive species mechanics
Social & Intrigue strike/presence feats that may depend on morale, fear, Persuasion, or condition-track workflows
Skills feats that may be combat-adjacent but primarily alter skill usage or skill rerolls

recommendedNextImplementationPools.why: Highest confidence because taxonomy already places them in Combat.
Likely maps to attack options, weapon qualities, proficiency metadata, or full-attack sequencing.
Many map to vehicle gunnery, vehicle defense, or vehicle action riders.
Likely maps to droid-specific attack, defense, repair, or tactical action riders.

caveats: The broad total is intentionally conservative-actionable, not every feat with a combat-sounding name in the full 401-feat taxonomy.
Generated backlog files may still show stale statuses; this audit subtracts only the explicit implementation addendum count.
Some feats in the broad remaining count may already have older partial implementations that still need runtime verification before they can be subtracted.
